inputs.label: Total Jobs Completed
inputs.businessContext: Total number of repair jobs completed in the period. Used to normalize comeback frequency.

inputs.label: Comeback Jobs
inputs.businessContext: Number of jobs that required rework or additional service due to incomplete or incorrect repair. Directly drives comeback cost.

inputs.label: Average Job Value
inputs.businessContext: Average revenue per job. Used to estimate lost revenue from rework.

inputs.label: Diagnostic Time per Comeback
inputs.businessContext: Average technician hours spent diagnosing the root cause of a comeback. Drives diagnostic labor cost.

inputs.label: Rework Time per Comeback
inputs.businessContext: Average technician hours spent performing the actual rework. Drives rework labor cost.

inputs.label: Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Shop's hourly labor charge rate. Used to calculate labor cost of diagnostic and rework time.

inputs.label: Parts Cost per Comeback
inputs.businessContext: Average cost of replacement parts used during rework. Includes handling and waste.

inputs.label: Customer Goodwill Cost per Comeback
inputs.businessContext: Estimated cost of discounts, vouchers, or compensation offered to retain customer after a comeback.

inputs.label: Comeback Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Calculated as (comebackJobs / totalJobs) * 100. Used for threshold-based warnings.

breakdown.comebackCost: Total direct cost of rework labor, parts, and goodwill
breakdown.diagnosticLeak: Cost of diagnostic time spent on comebacks
breakdown.summaryLevel: Risk level based on comeback rate

hiddenLossDrivers: comebackRatePercent >= 8 ? 'Critical comeback rate: immediate process improvement needed' : null
comebackRatePercent >= 4 && comebackRatePercent < 8 ? 'Warning: comeback rate above industry benchmark (4%)' : null
suggestedActions: Implement root cause analysis for comebacks
Enhance technician training on first-time fix
